title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Kew Gardens, Queens | $900-$2,500 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Kew Gardens, Queens. Safe, humane bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Kew Gardens residents facing bat infestations need professional help to safely remove these protected animals and prevent their return. Best Pest Control NYC offers comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Kew Gardens, ensuring your home or business is bat-free and properly sealed against future intrusions. Our experienced technicians understand the unique challenges posed by the historic homes and multi-family buildings common in this Queens neighborhood.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Kew Gardens for years with humane, effective bat removal that complies with all wildlife protection laws. Our licensed specialists use proven exclusion techniques tailored to the architectural diversity of Kew Gardens homes, from pre-war Tudor and Colonial revivals to modern apartment complexes, ensuring long-term protection and peace of mind.
localContext: Kew Gardens' tree-lined streets, proximity to Forest Park, and abundance of pre-war homes with attics and eaves create ideal conditions for bat colonization. The neighborhood's mix of single-family Tudor-style homes, co-ops along Queens Boulevard, and buildings near the historic Maple Grove Cemetery often provide the dark, sheltered spaces bats seek for roosting.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Kew Gardens?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Kew Gardens typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, the number of entry points, and the complexity of your property's architecture. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in New York?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bats are protected species in New York, so removal must be done humanely by licensed professionals. We use exclusion methods that allow bats to leave naturally but prevent re-entry, and we never perform removals during maternity season (May-August) when flightless pups are present.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Kew Gardens home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, visible bat droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, brown staining around openings, and seeing bats emerge from your home at sunset. Many Kew Gardens homeowners first notice bats flying around their rooflines during warm evenings.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our process includes a thorough inspection to locate all entry points and roosting areas, installation of one-way exclusion devices that let bats exit but not return, sealing all secondary entry points, and a follow-up visit to ensure complete exclusion. We also provide attic cleanup and sanitization services to remove guano and restore insulation if needed.
